name: Phone Call
description: Lets this agent place a real phone call for you — it dials out from the Kade-AI phone line, talks, and saves a transcript in Calls & Conversations. Calls are capped and recorded, and each one costs a little real money on the phone bill. It can't receive calls, only place them. Try: "call my pharmacy and ask if my refill is ready."

name: Push Notification
description: Lets this agent ping your phone's lock screen — reminders, check-ins, and "your long task is done" notes. It has built-in manners: quiet hours at night, cooldowns, and daily caps it cannot break. It can't text other people, only notify YOUR phone. Try: "remind me at 4 to take the bread out."

name: Voice Memo Transcriber
description: Turns an uploaded voice memo or audio recording into clean, formatted text. Attach the audio to the chat, then ask the agent to transcribe or summarize it. Free to use. There's also a standalone page at /transcribe.

name: Fal Studio (Video + Design)
description: Makes short AI video clips and polished design images (posters, cards, logos — the kind where the text comes out spelled right). Each video costs real money (roughly 50 cents to a dollar per short clip), design images a few cents. It can't edit videos you upload. Try: "make a 5-second video of a corgi surfing."

name: Calculator
description: Does exact math — arithmetic, percentages, conversions — so numbers are computed, not guessed. It can't graph or solve word problems by itself; it just crunches what the agent feeds it. Try: "what's 15% of 1,240?"

name: Flux
description: Draws brand-new pictures from a description, using the site's shared image credits. It can't edit or look at photos you upload — it only creates new ones. Try: "paint me a cozy cabin in the Ozarks at sunset."

authConfig.label: Your Flux API Key
authConfig.description: Provide your Flux API key from your user profile.

name: Weather
description: Looks up real, current weather and forecasts for any city — free and instant. It can't do past weather history. Try: "what's the weather in Springfield this weekend?"

name: Wikipedia
description: Looks things up on Wikipedia — people, places, history, science — so answers come from the encyclopedia instead of memory. Free. It only knows what Wikipedia knows. Try: "who was Bass Reeves?"

name: Jokes
description: Pulls fresh jokes from a live joke database — clean by default, uncensored only for adult personas. Try: "tell me a dad joke."

name: News briefing
description: Reads real, current headlines from free news feeds — national, world, Springfield/Ozarks local, tech, entertainment, music, sports. It gets headlines and summaries, not full articles. Try: "what's happening in the news this morning?"

name: Read a webpage
description: You share a link, it reads the page and returns just the article text — no ads, menus, or clutter. Great for having a webpage read aloud. It can't log into sites or fill out forms, and some paywalled pages won't open. Try: "read me this article" with a link pasted in.

name: Adventure save files
description: Gives story games a save file — so a text adventure or RPG can save, load, and pick up where you left off days later, even in a new conversation. Only useful for games. Try: "continue my dungeon adventure from last time."

name: Game Parlor
description: The Game Parlor: 19 voice-friendly games with a real referee keeping score — Blackjack, Uno, War, Go Fish, Farkle, Battleship, trivia, and more. The server tracks cards and dice, so the agent can't cheat or lose count. Try: "deal me some blackjack."

name: Send Feedback
description: Sends a bug report, feature idea, or general feedback straight to Kade, the person who runs this site. Free and instant. Try: "tell Kade the voice cut out during my call."

name: Family Messages
description: Takes a message for another person on this site — it's held and delivered next time they open a chat. It can't reach people outside Kade-AI (no texts or emails). Try: "tell Skylee I said happy birthday."

name: Code Runner
description: Runs a small computer program (Python, Bash, or Node) in a safe sandbox and reports what it prints — handy for exact calculations, date math, or quick data crunching. It can't reach the internet or your files. Try: "figure out how many days until Christmas."
